Petition For An Accounting Of Bypass Trust
Off Calendar Dec-16-2019 Continued To Dec-30-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-19-302990 The Konstin Family Revocable Trust and
PTR-19-302991 The Survivor's Trust of The Konstin Family Revocable Trust.

Appearances: Vincent Yu for Sydna Konstin.
Sheila Robello for John Konstin (Trustee).
Charles Jonas for Erin Markey.

Ms. Robello stated that all of the accountings have been provided except for the period of January 2018 to date of death, that she got it back last week, that it has been reviewed, that she tried to provide it, but the attachment did not go through and that right now the Parties have all of the accountings. Mr. Yu stated that his Client informed him that the CPA is on vacation and due to come back at the end of December, and that his Client indicated that there are some issues with the accounting and that she wants to talk with him. The Court clarified that it will not be reviewing or approving the accountings and that once the accountings are provided the petitions go away/are done. Mr. Jonas stated that Erin has incurred an additional $28,000.00 in fees and right now is providing services without pay. Ms. Robello stated that the special needs trust is entitled to a 50% interest in two rental properties, that the rental income is about $7,800.00 monthly, that there is no Court order indicating that these are special needs services, that they have agreed to pay some of those fees and that they have gotten consents for some of those payments. Ms. Robello also stated that she heard from an Attorney regarding a gift of a 15% interest in one of the apartments and that there is a dispute as final distribution and its context. The Petitions for Accountings, etc. (2) are continued to December 30, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For An Accounting Of Bypass Trust
Off Calendar Nov-18-2019 Continued To Dec-16-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-19-302990 The Bypass Trust of The Konstin Family Revocable Trust and PTR-19-302991 The Survivor's Trust of the Konstin Family Revocable Trust.

Appearances: Sheila Robello for John Konstin (Trustee) and specially appearing for Attorney John Rueppel on behalf of Sydna Konstin.
Anne Asis for Erin Markey Conservator of Eiven Pantoja (Beneficiary of the Special Needs Trust created in the Bypass Trust).

Mr. Robello stated that she needs to finalize one more accounting in both trusts for the period of January through July of 2018, that the accountings for 2015, 2016, 2017 and post death have been provided, that Mr. Rueppel has not had the opportunity to review the accountings and that Mr. Rueppel is asking for a continuance out to December 16th. Ms. Asis stated that she has no issue with a continuance, that her only concern was getting the accounting, that she received the accounting and has no issue with it and that it appears that the special needs trust has been funded. Ms. Asis also that Ms. Markey has petitioned for fees in the conservatorship, that fees have been ordered and that Ms. Markey is still waiting to be paid. Ms. Robello stated that they have been paying bills since October 1st, that they need to reconcile numbers, that they have not distributed the four pieces of properties, that the special needs trust was funded with income for two pieces of properties and that Ms. Markey will be paid. The accounting petitions (2) are continued to December 16, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For An Accounting Of Bypass Trust
Off Calendar Sep-23-2019 Continued To Nov-18-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-19-302990 The Bypass Trust of The Konstin Family Revocable Trust and PTR-19-302991 The Survivor's Trust of The Knostin Family Revocable Trust.

Appearances: John Rueppel for Sydna Konstin (Petitioner/Beneficiary).
Sheila Robello for John Konstin (Trustee).
Ann Asis for Erin Markey Conservator of Eivin Pantoja (Beneficiary of the Bypass Trust).

Ms. Robello stated that she filed a response on Thursday, that on September 3rd she provided an accounting from date of death (July 31, 2018) through June 30, 2019 for both trusts, and that while she disagrees with the legal authority to request accountings and believes the Petitioner is not entitled to an accounting, she has and will provide accountings in order to head off litigation. Ms. Robello also stated that she provided accountings for 2015 and for 2016 for both trusts, that she will be providing an accounting for 2017 and that she is working on 2018. Mr. Rueppel stated that it will take time to review the current accounting and the past accountings, and suggested putting the matter over for 60-days. Ms. Asis stated that she is okay with that, that she is just here to observe and that she is hopeful that the Parties are working this out and that the special needs trust will be funded by the end of this month. The Petition for an Accounting of the Bypass Trust is continued to November 18, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Rueppel is to file a status report by November 12, 2019.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For An Accounting Of Bypass Trust
Off Calendar Sep-09-2019 Continued To Sep-23-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
